title: Robopsychology: A Framework for Behavioral Diagnostics of AI Systems (reference CLI)
description:
Reference implementation of the Robopsychology framework: a behavioral diagnostic method for large language model systems that separates any observed output into three layers — model tendencies, runtime/host pressure, and conversation effects. The framework provides sixteen diagnostic prompts named after patterns in Asimov's robot stories, five operating rules, and a nine-step diagnostic ratchet that makes performed transparency expensive and genuine transparency cheap.

This deposit archives the robopsych command-line tool, the method documents (guide.md, method.md, taxonomy.md, related-work.md, deployment-contexts.md), and the reproducible validation cases. The canonical statement of the framework is the essay Robopsychology.

The accompanying paper draft (paper/) is included for transparency but is not yet submission-ready; quantitative validation of the ratchet across model families is an active research track tracked in the repository's issue queue.
